learning_outcomes.description: Describe the structure and function of the renal and endocrine systems and apply this knowledge to explain their roles in health and disease, including the effects of hormonal influences on bodily functions, and the consequences of dysfunction.
Be able to explain how thermoregulation is beneficial to the animal in temperature extremes and disease.

Compare and contrast the anatomy and physiology of non-domestic species including fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds, monotremes, marsupials, and small mammals with those of domestic animals, and apply this knowledge to explain how evolutionary pressures have shaped species differences and their clinical significance.
Apply the concepts of radiology to the generation of radiographic images. Use standard radiologic terminology to identify and describe normal anatomy of small animals and horses.
In the context of external industry learning, begin to develop a professional identity through reflection of pre-clinical WIL experience.

assessments.assessment_title: In Semester: Renal & Endocrine Theory Exam
assessments.task_description: A centrally-scheduled in-semester theory exam covering renal anatomy and physiology, and endocrine anatomy and physiology. The exam will incorporate a range of questions available in the Inspera examination platform. This is a 60-minute, in-person, invigilated exam run on the Gatton campus.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:

assessments.task_description:
Task Outline:

Reflective practice is a powerful approach to improving learning. Reflection is key as you move toward becoming a veterinary professional. This Reflection on your PC-WIL (in-clinic) experience allows you to reflect on your actions, beliefs, attitudes and learning, in the context of your knowledge relevant to the industry placement. Importantly, this reflective task will define a pathway to identifying weaknesses and consider what remediation could be undertaken, with view to consolidating your skills.

Task Requirement:

Students must complete a Reflection of Learning on the 10-day (PC-WIL) experience. The reflection must individually address the described professional expectations (1 – 5 listed below) in the context of your PC-WIL (in-clinic) industry placement.

Criteria & Marking:

·        The word limit is 1000 words, with a 200-word tolerance. 

·     A diverse approach to the format of the reflection is acceptable: i.e. you may use alternative forms of media such as PowerPoints, pictures, videos, text, tables, diagrams or a combination of these arrangements. (Please note – photos and videos only with the appropriate permission from the applicable industry partner).

·        The reflection will be marked based on the rubric provided on Blackboard. Personalised feedback on the reflection will be provided in the Grade Centre

·        The PC WIL Reflection must be graded as a PASS to achieve a passing grade for VETS2012.

Enquiries:

  •     Enquiries related to this Assessment Task should be directed to Rod Verrall, vetenquiries@uq.edu.au.  
  • PLEASE DO NOT SEND QUERIES TO THE COURSE COORDINATOR(S).


Professional Expectations:

1.     Reflect on the animal management of the enterprise encountered during the placement, including the animal husbandry practices observed and their contribution to animal health and welfare.

2.     Reflect on your personal and professional development throughout the placement, with consideration of your interpersonal skills and your proficiency in animal handling and restraint across a range of situations.

3.     Reflect on the professional approaches used to communicate and collaborate with clients, veterinary professionals, and other stakeholders during the PC-WIL placement.

4.     Demonstrate a basic understanding of the operation of the animal enterprise, including its business model, local risk factors, and relevant workplace health and safety considerations.

5.   Identify areas for personal and professional improvement and develop strategies to address self-identified learning needs and skill gaps.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The reflection must be submitted via the VETS2012 Blackboard site. Feedback will be provided and, in some instances, a re-submit requested by the WIL Coordinator.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

Maintain a reflective journal, which will serve as a record of AH/PC WIL experiences & capture your unique learning opportunities.
